violations.text: The door to the outside, located in the back of the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2026
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on prep unit 2 and on the two door stand up refrigeration unit. Gaskets must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2026
The floor in the kitchen was observed with broken tiles and areas of low grout. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2026
The hood system was observed with an excess amount of debris. Hood Systems must be maintained. New Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Can opener was washed and sanitized during inspection. Ice machine was emptied during inspection. Ice machine in not to be used until it is washed rinsed and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2024

Vegetable prep unit was observed held at 47 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Noodles in unit were 47 degrees, voluntarily disposed of. Vegetables had just finished being prepped and were moved to walk in cooler until unit is repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of staff, noodles was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Noodles were prepared two hours prior and were moved to freezer to rapid cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2024

The cutting boards in the kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 11/25/2024

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the dry storage area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 04/20/2023
